content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 13, 1991


Robert Booker
Chief Business and Financial Officer
Los Angeles Unified School District
Box 3307
Los Angeles, CA  90051

                                          Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                               Our File No. I-91-358

Dear Mr. Booker:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the filing obligations of a former member of the Los Angeles Unified School District Annuity Reserve Fund Board Member under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1  You have not provided us the name of the official whose duties under the Act are at issue.  Accordingly, we provide you with informal assistance pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 18329.2

QUESTION

     A member of the Los Angeles Unified School District Annuity Reserve Fund Board has resigned his position.  Is this retired member of the board required to file Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION

     The retired board member must file Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests.

_________________________

1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2    Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.  (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-91-358
Page No. 2

FACTS

On April 2, 1991, you requested our advice to determine
whether members of the Los Angeles Unified School District Annuity
Reserve Fund board (the "board") were required to file Commission
Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests. Your request for
advice was prompted by a memorandum from the Commission dated
December 5, 1990, which advised interested persons who had
requested information regarding Section 87200, as amended, of
their filing obligations under the statute. You were advised that
if you determined that members of the board managed public invest-
ments, they were required to file Commission Form 721 pursuant to
the provisions of Section 87200. (Booker Advice Letter, No. I-91-
227.)

You have determined that members of the board manage public
investments and are thus required to file Commission Form 721
Statements of Economic Interests. You now seek our advice to
determine whether a member of the board who retired prior to the
time when it was determined that members of the board were 87200
filers must file a Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of
Economic Interests.

ANALYSIS

As we have previously advised you, effective January 1, 1990,
Section 87200 was amended to add city treasurers, county treasur-
ers, and "other public officials who manage public investments" to
the list of persons who must file Commission Form 721 Statements
of Economic Interests. You have determined that members of the
board manage public investments. Accordingly, members of the
board are required to file, within 30 days of assuming office,
statements of economic interests disclosing their interests in
real property, investments, and income received during the 12
months before assuming office. (Section 87202.) For those posi-
tions added to Section 87200, as amended, this disclosure obliga-
tion commenced on January 1, 1990, which is the effective date of
the amendment.

Individuals subject to disclosure pursuant to the provisions
of Section 87200 must also file annual statements disclosing their
economic interests. (Section 87203.) In addition, these
individuals must file a statement within 30 days of leaving office
disclosing any investments, interests in real property, and income
received during the period since the previous statement filed
under Sections 87202 and 87203. (Section 87204.)

You have determined that members of the board are subject to
87200 disclosure, because they manage public investments. Thus,
the filing obligation of these officials arose at the time of the
effective date of Section 87200, as amended. Therefore, the
member of the board who resigned his position in October, 1990,
should have filed Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic
Interests while he was in office. Within 30 days of leaving

Our File No. I-91-358
Page No. 3


office, this official should have filed a Form 721 Leaving Office
Statement of Economic Interests.

     The Commission has attempted to clarify the statutory
language and enact regulations to define the term "other public
officials who manage public investments" as that term is used in
Section 87200. Pending adoption of pertinent regulations, staff
has consistently advised that public officials who manage public
investments must file Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic
Interests.  Therefore, because you have determined that members
of the board manage public investments and are subject to the
disclosure provisions of Section 87200, the member of the board
who owns resigned his position in October, 1990, must not only file a
leaving office statement pursuant to the provisions of Section
87204 but must also file an assuming office statement for the year
1990 pursuant to the provisions of Section 87202. This dual
filing obligation may be fulfilled by filing one Commission Form
721 and indicating, in the appropriate spaces, that this form is
both and assuming office and a leaving office statement.

     We recognize the difficulties which may arise in preparing
past statements of economic interests. However, statements of
economic interests are always filed at the end of a reporting
period and necessarily reflect past assets and income. In the
case of leaving office statements, they are routinely filed by
persons who are no longer public officials. One of the key
purposes of the Act is to provide through public disclosure of
the assets and income of public officials which may be materially
affected by their decisionmaking in order to avoid conflicts of
interest.

     We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.3

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Blanca M. Breeze
                                            Counsel, Legal Division


_______________
     3   Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: A member of the Los Angeles Unified School District Annuity Reserve Fund Board has resigned his position. Is this retired member of the board required to file Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion:
The retired board member must file Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests.

sections.facts:
On April 2, 1991, you requested our advice to determine whether members of the Los Angeles Unified School District Annuity Reserve Fund Board (the "board") were required to file Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests. Your request for advice was prompted by a memorandum from the Commission dated December 5, 1990, which advised interested persons who had requested information regarding Section 87200, as amended, of their filing obligations under the statute. You were advised that if you determined that members of the board managed public investments, they were required to file Commission Form 721 pursuant to the provisions of Section 87200. (Booker Advice Letter, No. I-91-227.)

You have determined that members of the board manage public investments and are thus required to file Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests. You now seek our advice to determine whether a member of the board who retired prior to the time when it was determined that members of the board were 87200 filers must file a Commission Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.analysis:
As we have previously advised you, effective January 1, 1990, Section 87200 was amended to add city treasurers, county treasurers, and "other public officials who manage public investments" to the list of persons who must file Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests. You have determined that members of the board manage public investments. Accordingly, members of the board are required to file, within 30 days of assuming office, statements of economic interests disclosing their interests in real property, investments, and income received during the 12 months before assuming office. (Section 87202.) For those positions added to Section 87200, as amended, this disclosure obligation commenced on January 1, 1990, which is the effective date of the amendment.

Individuals subject to disclosure pursuant to the provisions of Section 87200 must also file annual statements disclosing their economic interests. (Section 87203.) In addition, these individuals must file a statement within 30 days of leaving office disclosing any investments, interests in real property, and income received during the period since the previous statement filed under Sections 87202 and 87203. (Section 87204.)

You have determined that members of the board are subject to 87200 disclosure, because they manage public investments. Thus, the filing obligation of these officials arose at the time of the effective date of Section 87200, as amended. Therefore, the member of the board who resigned his position in October, 1990, should have filed Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests while he was in office. Within 30 days of leaving

office, this official should have filed a Form 721 Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests.

The Commission has attempted to clarify the statutory language and enact regulations to define the term "other public officials who manage public investments" as that term is used in Section 87200. Pending adoption of pertinent regulations, staff has consistently advised that public officials who manage public investments must file Commission Form 721 Statements of Economic Interests. Therefore, because you have determined that members of the board manage public investments and are subject to the disclosure provisions of Section 87200, the member of the board who resigned his position in October, 1990, must not only file a leaving office statement pursuant to the provisions of Section 87204 but must also file an assuming office statement for the year 1990 pursuant to the provisions of Section 87202. This dual filing obligation may be fulfilled by filing one Commission Form 721 and indicating, in the appropriate spaces, that this form is both an assuming office and a leaving office statement.

We recognize the difficulties which may arise in preparing past statements of economic interests. However, statements of economic interests are always filed at the end of a reporting period and necessarily reflect past assets and income. In the case of leaving office statements, they are routinely filed by persons who are no longer public officials. One of the key purposes of the Act is to provide thorough public disclosure of the assets and income of public officials which may be materially affected by their decisionmaking in order to avoid conflicts of interest.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.3
